The '''Hive Palace''' was a large fortress on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Cheelit/Legends|Cheelit]] that was owned by the [[J'feh/Legends|J'feh]] [[Lady/Legends|Lady]] [[Dhol]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It contained a chamber specially designed for the playing of the strategy game [[Firepath/Legends|firepath]]. The Hive Palace once hosted the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] after a firepath challenge from Lady Dhol, only for Dhol to hire a [[Guild of Vindicators]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] named [[Clat]] the Shamer to kill Vader. Clat left a trail of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] corpses across the grounds of the Hive Palace, but Vader anticipated Dhol's treachery and killed them both in the palace's firepath chamber.<ref name="dlc" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Hive Palace appeared in ''[[Dark Lord's Conscience]]'', a comic story written by [[Alan Moore]] and illustrated by [[John Stokes]], and originally released in [[1982]]'s [[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 155|''The Empire Strikes Back Monthly'' 155]].<ref name="dlc" />
